---
label: "Advanced Workflows"
icon: "🔧"
---

## Multi-Agent Workflows, MCP Integration, and Production Patterns

### Claude Code as a General-Purpose Agent

Claude Code is marketed as a coding tool, but it's actually a **general-purpose terminal agent** that happens to be exceptional at code. It can manage git workflows, run shell commands, interact with APIs, manage files, and orchestrate complex multi-step processes.

<CodeBlock language="text" label="Claude Code Beyond Coding" b64="" />

### MCP Servers: Extending Claude's Reach

<JargonTip term="MCP">Model Context Protocol</JargonTip> servers extend Claude Code's capabilities beyond the local filesystem. You can connect Claude to databases, APIs, cloud services, and custom tools.

<CodeBlock language="json" label="Claude Code MCP Configuration" b64="" />

### Skills vs MCP Servers: When to Use Which

<ComparisonTable title="Skills vs MCP Servers" columns={['Aspect','Skills','MCP Servers']} rows={[
  ['What they are','Text instructions Claude reads','Live services Claude calls'],
  ['Transparency','You can read and review the full text','Often black boxes — harder to audit'],
  ['Security','No external calls — just instructions','External service access — needs trust'],
  ['Best for','Consistent patterns, templates, procedures','External data, APIs, databases, tools'],
  ['Example','How to create a PPTX file','Connect to GitHub to read issues'],
]}/>

### Background Processes and Async Workflows

<CodeBlock language="text" label="Running Claude Code in Background" b64="" />

### Production Deployment Patterns

<CodeBlock language="text" label="CI/CD Integration with Claude Code" b64="" />

<AnalogyBox emoji="🏭" title="Automated Quality Gates">
Claude Code in CI/CD is like automated data quality checks in your MDM pipeline — every data record (PR) passes through validation (code review) before promotion to the golden record (main branch). The rules are defined once (CLAUDE.md + review criteria), and enforcement is automatic.
</AnalogyBox>

<Quiz
  question="You're building a SnapSense feature that needs to: (1) query Open Food Facts API, (2) store results in Hive, (3) update the UI. How should you structure the Claude Code session?"
  options={["One prompt: 'Build the complete feature'", "Plan Mode first, then implement in 3 separate steps with verification between each", "Ultrathink the entire feature in one pass", "Write the code yourself — this is too complex for Claude"]}
  correctIndex={1}
  explanation="Plan Mode identifies the architecture (API call → data transform → Hive storage → UI update). Then implementing step by step with verification catches issues at each layer boundary. Step 1 might reveal the API response format is different than expected — better to catch that before building the Hive storage layer on wrong assumptions."
/>

<SeeItInRe3 text="SnapSense uses GitHub Actions with a self-hosted macOS runner for CI/CD. Claude Code could be integrated into this pipeline for automated PR review and test generation." />
